figures.caption: Figure 1: An example from TimeBank-Dense (Cassidy et al., 2014), based on TimeBank 1.2 (Pustejovsky et al., 2006). Explicit signals link BEFORE(takeover, news) and SIMULTANEOUS(spent, thought), while spent-sold depends on implicit contextual cues.
Table 3: Comparison of F1-scores across four benchmark datasets: TimeBank-Dense (TBD), MATRES, TDDAuto, and TDDMan. The best performance for each dataset is highlighted in bold.
Figure 3: Effect of Top-K values on F1 performance across four datasets.

Figure 7: POS feature distribution of TDDAuto

Figure 4: Example from the TimeBank-Dense test set illustrating pair-conditioned top-K attention. The model predicts BEFORE between said (E1) and force (E2). Pair-level attention highlights connective cues (e.g., as, itself), while entity-level attention anchors event-specific tokens (e.g., visits, Saddam). The complementary patterns indicate structured evidence selection for temporal inference.

Figure 2: Architecture of WISTERIA. BERT and a transformer encoder generate contextualized representations, while pair-conditioned top-K cross-attention extracts key context for each entity pair. The biaffine, context, and pair-fusion outputs are integrated via late fusion for temporal relation classification.
